content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 ,I Street• Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Law Offices of 
D. Wayne Leech & Associates 
11001 East Valley Mall, Suite 200 
El Monte, CA 91731-2620 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-15-135 
Dear Mr. Leech: 
October I , 2015 
This correspondence is written confirmation that the Fair Political Practices Commission has 
withdrawn your July 24, 2015 request for advice under the provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
We took this step because we found that it was not possible for us to provide you with the 
assistance you requested. 
In an August 25, 2015 email, you stated that there are currently no specific upcoming 
decisions involving amendments to the Route 55 Corridor Specific Plan. You stated that the 
Glendora City Planning Commission is in the process of evaluating whether amendments to the 
Specific Plan should be recommended to the City Council. However, city staff does not yet know 
what amendments may be recommended. Without specific governmental decisions, we are unable 
to issue formal, written advice. Therefore, we have withdrawn your request. 
You may resubmit your request for advice once you have additional information on a 
specific decision. If you have any questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660. 
ER:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Emelyn Rodriguez 
Senior Commission Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: Whether certain amendments to the Route 55 Corridor Specific Plan by the Glendora City Planning Commission would implicate the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because no specific governmental decisions have been made yet; the request may be resubmitted once information about a specific decision is available.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The factual basis for withdrawal is explained in the body text. Request can be resubmitted with more specific information.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the advice request because no specific governmental decisions have been made yet; the request may be resubmitted once information about a specific decision is available.
